title: Kale Salad with Sweet Potatoes and Pomegranate Vinaigrette
ingredients: 1½ pounds sweet potatoes, peeled, quartered lengthwise, and cut crosswise into ½ inch pieces
12 oz kale, stemmed and sliced crosswise (7 cups)
½ head radicchio (5 oz), cored and sliced thin
½ cup pecans, toasted and chopped
instructions: Toss sweet potatoes with 1 Tbsp oil and season with salt and pepper.
Lay potatoes in a single layer on a rimmed baking sheet and roast until bottom edges are browned on both sides, 25–30 minutes, flipping potatoes halfway through roasting time.
Vigorously squeeze and massage kale with hands until leaves are uniformly darkened and slightly wilted, about 1 minute.
Whisk water, pomegranate molasses, shallot, honey, cider vinegar, ¼ tsp salt, and ¼ tsp pepper together in a large bowl.
While whisking constantly, drizzle in ⅓ cup oil.
Add roasted potatoes, kale, and radicchio to vinaigrette and toss gently to coat.
Transfer to platter and sprinkle with pecans and shaved Parmesan to taste.
notes: Recipe from America's Test Kitchen Complete Vegetarian Cookbook
Massaging the kale makes a huge difference in the final texture and flavor
Recipe given by Julie Melini, a fellow forensic nurse
